ticket.title: Indexer Pass 3: TS docs + cross-file Imports/Calls + JSON/YAML
TSDoc extraction in yah-kg-ts so /** @yah:tag(...) */ on TS items isn't silently ignored
next_steps: Walk comment siblings of each declaration in the tree-sitter walker (~50-100 lines)
Attach extracted doc strings to nodes the same way the Rust indexer does, so yah-kg-anno picks them up

ticket.title: Rust cross-file Imports edges: parse use statements, resolve super::/crate:: against module tree
next_steps: Build a per-module import map
Resolve use paths against workspace mod tree (super::, crate::)
Emit Imports edges between modules

ticket.title: TS cross-file Imports edges: parse import statements, resolve via tsconfig paths or relative
next_steps: Honor tsconfig.json paths + baseUrl when resolving non-relative specifiers
Relative specifiers resolve against the importer's directory

ticket.title: Best-effort Calls edges (Rust + TS): walk function bodies, emit when callee resolves unambiguously
next_steps: Skip ambiguous resolves rather than emit wrong edges
Test fixture: hand-pick a few clear single-resolution call sites and assert edge presence

ticket.title: yah-kg-json-yaml crate using DocKind::Anchor/Property/SchemaRef
next_steps: serde_yaml + serde_json indexer; emit Anchor for keys, Property for values, RefersTo/ConformsTo for cross-doc refs
Day-one targets: package.json, tsconfig.json, Cargo.toml, tauri.conf.json so config files appear in the architecture tab

ticket.title: TS cross-file Imports edges: parse import statements, resolve via tsconfig paths or relative
next_steps: Honor tsconfig.json paths + baseUrl when resolving non-relative specifiers
Relative specifiers resolve against the importer's directory

after: yah-kg-json-yaml crate landed: JsonIndexer + YamlIndexer in workspace, 16 tests pass (cargo test -p yah-kg-json-yaml). Day-one fixtures covered: package.json, tsconfig.json, tauri.conf.json. Property/Document/Anchor/SchemaRef nodes emit; $ref/$schema/extends produce RefersTo/ConformsTo edges that resolve cross-file once the target Document exists in the store. Whole 520-test workspace still green.

before: serde_yaml + serde_json indexer; emit Anchor for keys, Property for values, RefersTo/ConformsTo for cross-doc refs
Day-one targets: package.json, tsconfig.json, Cargo.toml, tauri.conf.json so config files appear in the architecture tab
after: serde_yaml + serde_json indexer; emit Anchor for keys, Property for values, RefersTo/ConformsTo for cross-doc refs
Day-one targets: package.json, tsconfig.json, Cargo.toml, tauri.conf.json so config files appear in the architecture tab
Cargo.toml support: needs Lang::Toml variant in yah-kg/src/kind.rs + a TomlIndexer in this crate (or split into yah-kg-toml). Skipped here because no Lang::Toml exists yet.
YAML anchor detection is regex-based (known false positives like '2 * 3' registering '3' as alias). Swap to a YAML event-stream parser (yaml-rust2 or libyaml-safer) for clean anchor/alias spans.
Property nodes carry the whole-file span — serde_json/serde_yaml strip positions. Tree-sitter pass for per-key spans would let lookup-by-line click into nested config keys.

after: yah-kg-json-yaml crate landed: JsonIndexer + YamlIndexer in workspace, 16 tests pass (cargo test -p yah-kg-json-yaml). Day-one fixtures covered: package.json, tsconfig.json, tauri.conf.json. Property/Document/Anchor/SchemaRef nodes emit; $ref/$schema/extends produce RefersTo/ConformsTo edges that resolve cross-file once the target Document exists in the store. Lang::Toml + TomlIndexer landed in yah-kg-json-yaml (Cargo.toml fixture covers File/Document/nested Property emission; 4 new tests). YAML anchor detection rewritten on yaml-rust2's Scanner token stream — fixes the '2 * 3' false positive and gets accurate per-token line markers from libyaml-grade scanning. Workspace: 524 tests pass (was 520). Single ticket annotation now lives at yah-kg-json-yaml/src/lib.rs:59 (the kind.rs duplicate was stripped).

Tree-sitter pass for per-key Property spans — serde_json / serde_yaml / toml::Value all strip source positions, so Property nodes still carry file-wide spans. A precise walker would let the architecture tab click into nested config keys.

Wire JsonIndexer / YamlIndexer / TomlIndexer into the IndexerRegistry. Today only RustIndexer + TsIndexer are registered (app/tauri/src/state.rs:331 and yah-kg-daemon/tests/e2e.rs); JSON/YAML/TOML indexing is unit-tested but not yet hot-path.

before: yah-kg-json-yaml crate landed: JsonIndexer + YamlIndexer in workspace, 16 tests pass (cargo test -p yah-kg-json-yaml). Day-one fixtures covered: package.json, tsconfig.json, tauri.conf.json. Whole 520-test workspace still green.

Lang::Toml + TomlIndexer landed in yah-kg-json-yaml (Cargo.toml fixture covers File/Document/nested Property emission; 4 new tests). YAML anchor detection rewritten on yaml-rust2's Scanner token stream — fixes the '2 * 3' false positive and gets accurate per-token line markers from libyaml-grade scanning. Workspace: 524 tests pass (was 520). Single ticket annotation now lives at yah-kg-json-yaml/src/lib.rs:59 (the kind.rs duplicate was stripped).
after: Json + Yaml + Toml indexers shipped in yah-kg-json-yaml. Lang::Toml added to yah-kg::kind; YAML anchor scan now rides yaml-rust2's Scanner token stream (fixes the '2 * 3' false positive, gets libyaml-grade per-token line markers). 524-test workspace green; 20 tests in this crate. Per-key span tracking is the only remaining native limitation.

ticket.title: Wire JsonIndexer/YamlIndexer/TomlIndexer into the daemon's IndexerRegistry
next_steps: app/tauri/src/state.rs:331 currently registers only RustIndexer + TsIndexer; add Json/Yaml/Toml so config files appear in the architecture tab at runtime, not just in unit tests.
Mirror the same registration in yah-kg-daemon/tests/e2e.rs to cover the multi-language walk.

before: Json + Yaml + Toml indexers shipped in yah-kg-json-yaml. Lang::Toml added to yah-kg::kind; YAML anchor scan now rides yaml-rust2's Scanner token stream (fixes the '2 * 3' false positive, gets libyaml-grade per-token line markers). 524-test workspace green; 20 tests in this crate. Per-key span tracking is the only remaining native limitation.
after: Json + Yaml + Toml indexers shipped in yah-kg-json-yaml. Lang::Toml added to yah-kg::kind; YAML anchor scan now rides yaml-rust2's Scanner token stream (fixes the '2 * 3' false positive, gets libyaml-grade per-token line markers). 524-test workspace green; 20 tests in this crate. Per-key span tracking is the only remaining native limitation.

JSON Property nodes now carry per-key spans via a side-band tree-sitter-json pass (yah-kg-json-yaml/src/spans.rs). Walker grew a SpanLookup trait that JsonIndexer wires with PointerSpans; YamlIndexer/TomlIndexer pass NoSpans and still fall back to file-wide. New deps: tree-sitter 0.23 + tree-sitter-json 0.24. Tests: 11 unit + 18 integration in this crate (added json_property_nodes_carry_per_key_spans + yaml_and_toml_properties_still_use_file_wide_span_until_followup as a guard for the next pass). Whole workspace green.

before: Tree-sitter pass for per-key Property spans — serde_json / serde_yaml / toml::Value all strip source positions, so Property nodes still carry file-wide spans. A precise walker would let the architecture tab click into nested config keys.
after: Tree-sitter pass for per-key Property spans — serde_json / serde_yaml / toml::Value all strip source positions, so Property nodes still carry file-wide spans. A precise walker would let the architecture tab click into nested config keys.

Wire YAML per-key spans via yaml-rust2's Parser event stream — yaml-rust2 is already a dep, and EventReceiver callbacks expose Marker(line, col) on every scalar/mapping start. Build a PointerSpans by walking events in parallel with the value tree; pass into Walker::with_spans from YamlIndexer.

Wire TOML per-key spans via toml_edit (already in our dep tree at 0.22 transitively through toml=0.8). DocumentMut exposes span() on each item; walk recursively to populate PointerSpans, then pass into Walker::with_spans from TomlIndexer. Note: switching the parser from toml::Value → toml_edit::DocumentMut means refactoring toml_to_json to consume the edit form.
Once YAML/TOML span sources land, flip yaml_and_toml_properties_still_use_file_wide_span_until_followup to assert per-key spans (the test name + comment already mark it as a tripwire).

ticket.title: YAML per-key spans via yaml-rust2 Parser events
